sourceMovie.title: King Arthur
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto King Arthur (2004) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and war blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Kingdom of Heaven
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If King Arthur clicked for you, Kingdom of Heaven (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and war, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after King Arthur?
faq.answer: Try Kingdom of Heaven, The Messenger: The Story of Joan of Arc, Braveheart—each shares DNA with King Arthur while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is King Arthur?
faq.answer: King Arthur (2004) is best described as Adventure, War, History, Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is King Arthur worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
